topic: GitHub Copilot Coding Agent Guide 2026: Autonomous Background Task Agent

competitors.title: GitHub Copilot Coding Agent vs Claude Code: Background Agents vs Interactive Agents
key_points: GitHub Copilot coding agent is a background worker: assign task via GitHub Issue, opens PR, review result
Claude Code is an interactive partner: work alongside in terminal with real-time feedback
Copilot agent workflow: assign issue → creates branch → autonomous implementation → PR for review → iteration via PR comments
Copilot excels at low-complexity issues and volume processing: assign 10 issues, review 10 PRs
Key difference: Copilot feedback loop is hours (assign → PR → review), Claude Code feedback loop is seconds (describe → execute → adjust)
Copilot agent inherently parallel, can work on multiple issues simultaneously in cloud environments
Copilot agent limitations: cannot ask clarifying questions during execution, limited to GitHub-hosted repos, struggles with ambiguous requirements

competitors.title: Claude Code vs GitHub Copilot vs Cursor (2026): Honest Comparison
key_points: GitHub Copilot coding agent included with Enterprise ($39/user/month) or usage-based on other plans
Pricing tiers: Copilot Pro $10/mo (300 premium requests), Pro+ ~$39/mo (1,500 premium), Business $19/user/mo, Enterprise $39/user/mo (1,000 premium/user)
Copilot's advantage: broad IDE support (VS Code, JetBrains, Neovim, Xcode, Eclipse, Zed) and model flexibility (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, xAI)
Enterprise features: custom knowledge bases, PR summaries, fine-grained admin controls, IP indemnity
Free tier functionality: 2,000 completions and 50 chat/agent requests per month, no credit card required
Premium request limits: 300/mo on Pro, 1,500/mo on Pro+, overages at $0.04/request add up quickly on agentic tasks

competitors.title: GitHub Copilot Agent Mode: The Complete Guide for 2026
key_points: Agent mode vs Ask mode vs Edit mode: Agent mode makes multi-file changes, runs terminal commands, uses MCP/linters, auto-fixes errors, plans multi-step tasks
Coding agent (cloud-based via GitHub Actions) creates branches, writes code, runs tests, opens PRs — requires Pro+ or Enterprise
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration lets agent mode query databases, read Confluence/Notion docs, check CI/CD status, pull Figma specs
copilot-setup-steps.yml configures the coding agent's development environment in GitHub Actions
Security: coding agent auto-runs CodeQL analysis, secret scanning, dependency review, supply chain checks before creating PRs
Key limitation: coding agent does NOT respect Copilot content exclusions — can still read and modify excluded files

stats.fact: GitHub Copilot has ~20M total users (July 2025), 4.7M paid subscribers (Jan 2026, ~75% YoY growth)
Copilot used by ~90% of Fortune 100 companies, 77K enterprise customers (FY2024)
Copilot suggestion acceptance rate ~30%, premium requests cost $0.04 each after allocation
AI code tools market projected from $4.86B (2023) to $26B by 2030
Copilot coding agent requires GitHub Copilot Enterprise ($39/user/month) or Pro+ plan

stats.fact: Agent mode is GA in 2026 — no preview flags needed, works out of the box with VS Code Copilot extension
